title: Health Economics & Outcomes Research
description: The HEOR module provides analytical tools for health economics and outcomes research, enabling evaluation of treatment value, healthcare resource utilisation, and patient outcomes. Researchers can conduct cost-effectiveness analyses, burden of disease studies, and comparative effectiveness research using real-world data.
tips: Define clinically meaningful outcome endpoints and relevant comparator treatments before configuring your HEOR analysis.
Use cohort characterisation results to describe baseline patient characteristics and support propensity score adjustment in comparative analyses.
Incorporate healthcare utilisation domains such as inpatient visits, procedures, and drug exposures to capture total cost of care.
Validate your real-world evidence findings against published clinical trial benchmarks to assess external validity.
Engage health economics collaborators early to ensure that model structures and assumptions align with payer and regulatory expectations.
